result:
**Proposed goal: Add true color (24-bit RGB) rendering support to replace the 256-color palette, dramatically improving visual fidelity across all 132 modes.**

Currently every mode is constrained to 256 terminal colors. Modern terminals (iTerm2, Kitty, Alacritty, Windows Terminal, GNOME Terminal) support 24-bit color via `\033[38;2;R;G;Bm` escape sequences. This single change would:

- Give all physics/fluid/chemical modes smooth, continuous color gradients instead of banded palettes
- Enable perceptually uniform colormaps (viridis, magma, inferno) for scientific accuracy
- Make fractal, reaction-diffusion, and wave modes visually stunning
- Auto-detect terminal capability and fall back to 256-color gracefully

This is high-leverage — one rendering layer improvement that instantly upgrades every existing mode without touching their logic.
